story: Makula tracked the slayer of Lillus through new snow, ignoring a doe and crossing an ice-choked river. He found scent marks and pressed on as dusk fell. A winter-hare died in an iron jaw in the scrub; he moved on. The moon rose. Smoke came first, then the glow of a campfire in a knot of firs. Earlier, the slayer had scooped a pit by the fire and roofed it with twigs and fresh snow, a mouth waiting for whatever charged straight in. Ears flat, body low, Makula slipped between trees and saw the man alone.
implausible_knowledge: Earlier, the slayer had scooped a pit by the fire and roofed it with twigs and fresh snow, a mouth waiting for whatever charged straight in.
question: As Makula closes on the campfire and sees the slayer alone, what will he do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Circle wide to test and skirt the ground by the fire, avoiding a straight charge.
answer_without_knowledge: Launch a direct spring from cover toward the man by the fire.
